---
pagetitle: "Agentic Basics"
title: "[Agentic Basics]{.amber}"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#2C3E50"
  data-background-image: figs/banner.png
  data-background-size: 24%
  data-background-position: right 20% center
  class: dark-centered
  style: text-align:left;
---

## About {.center style="font-size: 30px; line-height: 1.5;"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
- **Pingfan Hu**
- PhD Candidate at George Washington University
- Heavy Claude Code user since release
- Blog series: *Agentic Engineering* parts 1–3
- [pingfanhu.com](http://pingfanhu.com)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
- **Dr. John Helveston**
- Associate Professor at George Washington University
- Open-source author and educator
- [jhelvy.com](https://www.jhelvy.com)
:::
:::

## Today {.light-centered .center}

Three sections, ~25 minutes:

1. **[Assistants]{.teal} vs [Agents]{.amber}** — the vocabulary
2. **Architecture** — what's inside Claude Code, and how to wire it
3. **Handy Tools** — software, extensions, and habits that make it work

> Goal: walk out with the mental model to design your *own* workflow.

## [Section 1]{.amber}<br>Assistants vs Agents {.dark-centered .center}

## The opening problem {.light-centered .center}

- AI moves faster than anyone can keep up.
- New model, new tool, new workflow — every morning.
- The reframe:

::: {.centered-container style="width: 70%; margin-top: 1em;"}
You don't need to chase every release.<br>You need a workflow that **works for you**.
:::

<br>

> *"Everyone has their developing flow."* — Andrej Karpathy

## Two terms, one distinction {.light-centered .center}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [AI Assistants]{.teal}

- Conversational interfaces
- You ask → it answers
- ChatGPT, Claude.ai, Gemini
- Can browse, run code, remember
- **You drive each turn**
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [AI Agents]{.amber}

- Autonomous executors
- Run shell, read/write files, call APIs
- Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI
- Loop through decisions
- **Acts independently in an environment**
:::
:::

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 60%;"}
Assistants answer. Agents act.
:::

## What an "agent" actually is {.light-centered .center}

Anthropic's clearest definition:

::: {.centered-container style="width: 60%; font-size: 1.3em;"}
Agent = [LLM]{.teal} + [Tools]{.amber} + [Loop]{.purple}
:::

<br>

The loop:

1. LLM decides what tool to call
2. Tool runs in the environment
3. Result feeds back into context
4. LLM decides next move
5. Repeat

> Everything in this workshop is about shaping that loop.

## [Workflows]{.teal} vs [Agents]{.amber} {.light-centered .center}

From Anthropic's *Building Effective Agents*:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Workflows]{.teal}

- Predefined sequences of LLM calls
- Glued together by code
- **Control flow is fixed**
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Agents]{.amber}

- LLM decides the next step
- Dynamic decision-making
- **Control flow is dynamic**
:::
:::

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 70%;"}
Claude Code is firmly in the **agent** camp.
:::

## When to use which {.light-centered .center}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Use an [Assistant]{.teal} for...

- One-off questions
- Quick lookups
- Polishing a paragraph
- Anything that fits in one chat
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Use an [Agent]{.amber} for...

- Multi-step tasks
- Project work
- File or API operations
- Anything that loops
:::
:::

<br>

> Rest of the workshop assumes you're stepping up to **agents**, specifically Claude Code.

## [Section 2]{.amber}<br>Architecture {.dark-centered .center}

## Where you launch matters {.light-centered .center}

::: {.centered-container style="width: 75%;"}
The most important step is **picking the directory you launch from**.
:::

<br>

- That directory becomes the agent's environment
- Every file under it is potential context
- Two kinds of directories matter ↓

## Two kinds of directories {.light-centered .center}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Home]{.coral} `~`

- Computer-level tasks
- Convenient but **dangerous**
- Agent can touch anything
- Good for **global** `CLAUDE.md`
- Avoid launching real work here
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Project]{.sage} `cd <path>`

- Scoped to one project
- Own `CLAUDE.md`
- Own `.claude/` config
- Own conversation history
- **Where work actually lives**
:::
:::

## Quick install {.light-centered .center}

```bash
# install
brew install --cask claude-code

# change to project
cd /path/to/your-project

# launch
claude
```

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 70%;"}
Or open the project in an IDE (VS Code, Positron, Kaku)<br>and launch `claude` from the integrated terminal.
:::

## The four components {.light-centered .center}

Every Claude Code feature falls into one of these:

| Component | What it does | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **[Context]{.teal}** | What Claude reads at session start | `CLAUDE.md`, `rules/`, `memory/` |
| **[Capabilities]{.amber}** | What Claude can act on | MCP servers, plugins |
| **[Behaviors]{.coral}** | How Claude operates | skills, hooks |
| **[Agents]{.purple}** | Delegated execution | subagents, verifiers |

## [Context]{.teal} — the foundation {.light-centered .center}

- **`CLAUDE.md`** — auto-loaded every session. Project root + subdirs. Keep it short.
- **Global `CLAUDE.md`** at `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` — applies to *every* session.
- **`.claude/rules/*.md`** — topical knowledge, loaded **on demand**.
- **`memory/`** — auto-managed, never pushed to cloud, don't worry about it.

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 70%;"}
Trigger to write a rule: *"I want this reproduced reliably."*
:::

## [Capabilities]{.amber} — what Claude acts on {.light-centered .center}

- **MCP** (Model Context Protocol) — external servers
  - GitHub, Gmail, Figma, browser automation, calendars, databases
  - Claude calls them as structured tools
- **Plugins** — installable bundles
  - Skills + MCPs + hooks + config in one package
  - Install via `/plugin`

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 60%;"}
⚠️ MCPs eat tokens fast. Use sparingly.
:::

## [Behaviors]{.coral} — how Claude operates {.light-centered .center}

- **Skills** — reusable slash commands
  - `/commit`, `/review`, `/init`
  - Stored as `.md` files
  - Auto-trigger on context, or manual `/name`

- **Hooks** — shell commands fired on events
  - Linter after every edit
  - Formatter on save
  - Build verification at session end

> Part 2 zooms into skill design.

## [Agents]{.purple} — delegated execution {.light-centered .center}

- **Subagents** — child Claude instances
  - Focused subtask, separate context
  - Keeps the main session clean
  - Run in parallel for big problems

- **Verifiers** — post-execution checks
  - A test, a diff, a log review
  - Confirms output before "done"

> Part 3 builds out the full multi-agent **harness**.

## A concrete project layout {.light-centered .center}

```text
your-project/
├── CLAUDE.md                  # rules loaded every session
├── .claude/
│   ├── rules/                 # topical, on-demand
│   │   ├── core.md
│   │   ├── config.md
│   │   └── release.md
│   ├── skills/                # slash commands
│   ├── agents/                # subagent definitions
│   └── settings.json          # hooks, permissions
└── docs/
    └── ai/                    # human + AI shared docs
```

> The shape varies. The point: every piece has a home.

## Plan first, then act {.light-centered .center}

The single biggest behavioral lever:

- For any non-trivial task → enter **plan mode** first
- Make Claude write a plan
- Agree on it
- *Then* implement
- If things go sideways → **stop and re-plan**, don't push through

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 70%;"}
The model is good. Letting it plan first makes it much better.
:::

## The bigger picture {.dark-centered .center}

Three terms, three levels of maturity. They **coexist**.

| Stage | What it is |
|---|---|
| **[Vibe Coding]{.teal}** | Describe → generate → hope. Unpredictable. |
| **[Agentic Engineering]{.amber}** | Rules + skills → reproducible workflows. *(this workshop)* |
| **[Harness Engineering]{.coral}** | Structured environment around the model. *(Part 3)* |

<br>

> Stop handcrafting every piece. Start conducting.

## [Section 3]{.amber}<br>Handy Tools {.dark-centered .center}

## Don't panic {.light-centered .center}

- *"10 must-install AI tools!"* — every week
- You install them → they disappear from your life
- **There is no must-install kit.**
- General-purpose tools help. Most of what you need is buildable.

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 75%;"}
The tools below are the ones that actually stuck.
:::

## Software stack {.light-centered .center}

Four daily-use tools. Each replaceable; the categories aren't.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
- **[Obsidian]{.teal}** — notes as `.md`, free iCloud sync
- **[Spokenly]{.amber}** — speech-to-text, multilingual
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
- **[Positron]{.coral}** — IDE from Posit, VS Code base
- **[Kaku]{.purple}** — open-source macOS terminal
:::
:::

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 80%;"}
Pattern: small, configurable, integrates with Claude Code via text files or terminal.
:::

## Extensions taxonomy {.light-centered .center}

No official name. Three kinds:

- **[Plugins]{.teal}** — marketplace bundles via `/plugin`
  - `everything-claude-code`, `claude-plugins-official`
- **[MCPs (connectors)]{.amber}** — external servers
  - Excalidraw, Figma, TinyFish, Gmail, Calendar
- **[Skills]{.coral}** — slash commands as `.md` files
  - tw93's [Waza](https://github.com/tw93/waza): `/think`, `/design`, `/hunt`, `/check`, `/write`, `/learn`, `/read`, `/health`

<br>

> Two design principles preview: **less is more** + **negative examples** *(Part 2)*

## Custom shell commands {.light-centered .center}

Not AI tools — but what makes the AI workflow frictionless.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### `q-` (quick)

- `q-brew` — update homebrew
- `q-pull` — pull all repos
- `q-hide` — hide windows
- `q-close` — close apps
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### `cd-` (change dir)

- `cd-obsidian` → vault
- `cd-website` → website
- `cd-github` → repos root
:::
:::

<br>

> Each is one line. Cheap to add. Real time saved every day.

## The actual hardest skill {.dark-centered .center}

> *"Your success in life will be determined largely by your ability to speak, your ability to write, and the quality of your ideas — in that order."*
>
> — Patrick Winston, MIT AI Lab

<br>

::: {.centered-container style="width: 75%;"}
**Old:** *"Talk is cheap, show me the code."*<br>
**New:** *"Code is cheap, show me the prompt."*
:::

## Three takeaways {.light-centered .center}

1. **Assistants vs Agents** — agents act in an environment. Agent = LLM + tools + loop.

2. **Architecture** — working directory is scope. Four components: **Context, Capabilities, Behaviors, Agents**. Plan first.

3. **Handy Tools** — no must-install kit. Pick small composable tools. Use plugins/MCPs/skills sparingly. The hardest skill is still speaking clearly.

## Up next {.dark-centered .center}

### [Part 2:]{.amber} Skill Usage and Design

- Common skills people actually use
- How to design your own (`SKILL.md` anatomy, the Gotchas section)
- Sharing skills across projects with symlinks
